evals.expected_output: Scans today or tomorrow's calendar (based on time of day), creates meeting notes for deal meetings, updates daily notes
expectations: Config read from ld-config.md, calendar_configured checked
Current time determined to select today (morning) or tomorrow (afternoon)
Events fetched from Google Calendar via mcp__claude_ai_Google_Calendar__gcal_list_events
All-day events, declined events, cancelled events, and no-attendee events filtered out
Account folders listed and matched against event titles and attendee domains
Events classified as deal meeting, deal prep, internal, or external non-deal
Deal meeting files created under correct account meetings/ folder with full template
Attendees populated from calendar event display names, user's own email excluded
Daily note updated with full wiki-link paths and 4-item checklist
Existing meeting files not overwritten
Unrecognized external meetings listed with suggested /ld-create-account commands
Report table shows deal meetings, prep, internal, skipped, and unrecognized

evals.expected_output: Scans Monday through Friday of the current week, creates meeting notes for all days
expectations: Date range covers Monday through Friday of the current week
Meeting files created for all matching events across the week
Daily notes only updated for today and future days (past days skipped)
Multiple events on the same day for the same account get separate files with distinguishing topics

evals.expected_output: Scans Monday through Friday of the following week
expectations: Date range covers Monday through Friday of the FOLLOWING week
All meeting files created in advance
Daily notes updated for each future day
Date range covers only 2026-04-15
Events fetched for that single day

evals.expected_output: Scans tomorrow regardless of time of day
expectations: Target date is tomorrow regardless of current time
Events fetched for tomorrow only

evals.expected_output: Extracts agenda from calendar event description, strips video conferencing boilerplate, populates meeting note Agenda section
expectations: Event description field checked for substantive content
Zoom/Teams/Google Meet join links, dial-in numbers, and passcodes stripped
HTML tags stripped and content converted to plain text
Meaningful agenda items, discussion topics, and document links preserved
Extracted agenda inserted under ## Agenda section in the created meeting file

evals.prompt: /ld-calendar (deal meeting with MEDDPICC gaps and competitor mentions)
evals.expected_output: Generates targeted questions based on framework gaps and competitive intelligence, populates Questions to Ask section
expectations: Account file MEDDPICC, TECHMAPS, and Command of the Message sections read
Empty or thin MEDDPICC fields generate discovery questions
Missing TECHMAPS technical details generate architecture questions
Questions tailored to meeting type (discovery vs demo vs POV review)
Competitor mentions in account Ledger and Competition section cross-referenced
Competitive question added if competitor previously mentioned in account
ld-config.md learnings checked for [competitor] patterns from other accounts
Questions formatted as bullet points under ### Questions to Ask section

evals.expected_output: Only creates meeting notes for deal meetings and deal prep, skips internal and external non-deal events
expectations: calendar_mode read from config as 'deals'
Deal meetings processed and meeting files created
Deal prep meetings processed if calendar_include_prep is true
Internal meetings skipped entirely (no files created, no daily note entries)
External non-deal meetings skipped entirely
Report shows Internal section as 'Skipped -- deals-only mode'

evals.prompt: /ld-calendar (two events same account same day same topic)
evals.expected_output: Creates separate meeting files with numbered disambiguation when account, topic, and date collide
expectations: First event creates YYYY-MM-DD Discovery.md
Second event with identical account+topic+date creates YYYY-MM-DD Discovery 2.md
Both files get separate daily note entries
Events with 'Busy' summary are filtered out and not counted

evals.prompt: /ld-calendar (legacy config with calendar_accounts list instead of calendar_id)
evals.expected_output: Uses first account from legacy list and warns user to re-run /ld-setup calendar
expectations: Detects calendar_accounts (list) instead of calendar_id (string) in config
Uses the first account in the list as the calendar ID
Note shown to user recommending /ld-setup calendar to update config format
